document_title: AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026
extraction_scope: Only numbered manifesto points were included. Non-point narrative text such as preamble and conclusion was excluded.
hierarchy_convention: Each point includes hierarchy_down_to_top ordered from the point itself upward to subsection/section/document.

points.text: Special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- to every family to manage the tax burden and price rise: Under the administratively inefficient Vidiya DMK government led by Stalin, the prices of essential commodities have skyrocketed like poison over the past five years. Consequently, the public has been severely affected. Similarly, various taxes such as property tax, house tax, electricity charges, and water tax have been hiked. As a result, the daily financial burden on families has multiplied. Therefore, to reduce this burden on the people, a special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- will be provided to every family.
points.title: Special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- to every family to manage the tax burden and price rise

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- to every family to manage the tax burden and price rise
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: EPS promises Rs 10,000 'karunai thogai' for all families
evidence.snippet: AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami announced a one-time grant of Rs 10,000 to every family in the state to offset the financial burden caused by price hike and increase in taxes.

plan_existence.notes: While the government has provided one-time relief for specific events (e.g., Rs 6,000 for flood relief or Rs 1,000 for Pongal), no universal 'tax burden' assistance of Rs 10,000 exists. This is a new, large-scale populist proposal.

fiscal.comments: With approximately 2.24 crore ration cards in Tamil Nadu, a one-time payment of Rs 10,000 would cost roughly Rs 22,400 crore. This represents nearly 7-8% of the state's total revenue receipts, significantly straining an already debt-stressed exchequer (projected debt of Rs 10.71 lakh crore by 2027).

legal.comments: Direct cash transfers are legally permissible under the state's executive powers for welfare.

administrative.comments: Tamil Nadu has a robust Public Distribution System (PDS) and Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) infrastructure capable of executing such a payout quickly.

timeline.comments: As a one-time grant, it can be delivered within the first year of a 5-year term.

political.comments: High public reception expected as it targets every household regardless of income, though it may face criticism as 'freebie' politics.

feasibility.overall_comments: The scheme is administratively simple but fiscally reckless. While it provides immediate relief to citizens, the massive one-time drain on the state budget could cripple long-term capital expenditure.
key_risks: Severe fiscal deficit expansion
Inflationary pressure due to sudden liquidity injection
Diversion of funds from infrastructure or healthcare

points.text: 'Kula Vilakku Scheme' to protect women's welfare: To create economic equality in society, a monthly assistance of Rs. 2,000/- will be provided to all family cardholders through the Kula Vilakku Scheme. This amount will be deposited directly into the bank account of the female head of the family.
points.title: 'Kula Vilakku Scheme' to protect women's welfare

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 'Kula Vilakku Scheme' to protect women's welfare
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Magalir Urimai Thogai scheme expanded to cover 1.3 crore women
evidence.snippet: The current scheme provides Rs 1,000 per month to over 1.3 crore women heads of families with an annual income below Rs 2.5 lakh.

plan_existence.notes: The 'Kula Vilakku Scheme' is a direct expansion and rebranding of the existing KMUT. It doubles the monthly benefit from Rs 1,000 to Rs 2,000 and aims to cover 'all' family cardholders, removing current income/eligibility caps.

fiscal.comments: The current KMUT (Rs 1,000 for 1.3 crore women) costs ~Rs 13,000 crore annually. Doubling the amount to Rs 2,000 and expanding to all 2.24 crore cardholders would cost approximately Rs 53,760 crore per year, which is nearly 20% of the state's annual revenue.

legal.comments: No legal hurdles; it is a standard welfare entitlement.

administrative.comments: The mechanism for identifying female heads and bank account linking is already fully operational under the current government.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented immediately by increasing the existing DBT transfer amount.

political.comments: Extremely high political appeal; women voters are a decisive demographic in Tamil Nadu.

feasibility.overall_comments: While politically potent and administratively ready, the fiscal burden is unsustainable without massive new revenue streams or cutting other essential services.
key_risks: Unsustainable recurring expenditure
Crowding out of capital investment
Potential for increased state debt

points.text: Free bus travel scheme for men, similar to women: A free bus travel scheme will be implemented for men traveling in city buses. Furthermore, the existing free bus travel scheme for women in city buses will continue to be implemented.
points.title: Free bus travel scheme for men, similar to women

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Free bus travel scheme for men, similar to women
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

existing_scheme_names: Vidiyal Payanam (Free Bus Travel for Women)

evidence.title: EPS announces free bus travel for men
evidence.snippet: AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami announced that the ongoing free bus travel scheme for women would be extended to men as well.

plan_existence.notes: Currently, free travel in ordinary town buses is restricted to women, transgender persons, and persons with disabilities. Extending this to men would make public transport effectively free for all residents.

fiscal.comments: State Transport Undertakings (STUs) in TN are already in deep financial crisis. The current women's scheme costs ~Rs 3,600 crore in annual subsidies. Since men constitute the majority of paying passengers, extending free travel to them would eliminate almost all ticket revenue, requiring a total transport subsidy likely exceeding Rs 10,000 crore annually.

legal.comments: Legally feasible as a policy decision by the state government.

administrative.comments: Easily implemented using the existing 'zero-cost' ticket system used for women.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented on day one of a new government.

political.comments: Popular among low-income male workers, though it may be viewed as a 'freebie' that compromises bus quality and frequency.

feasibility.overall_comments: Administratively seamless but fiscally catastrophic for the transport sector. It risks the total collapse of STU financial independence, making them entirely dependent on state grants.
key_risks: Bankruptcy of State Transport Undertakings
Deterioration of bus maintenance and fleet expansion
Overcrowding leading to safety concerns

points.text: 'Amma Illam Scheme' providing houses for homeless families: Through the "Amma Illam Scheme," the government itself will purchase land and build free concrete houses for those in rural areas who do not own a house plot or a house. Similarly, for the homeless poor in urban areas, multi-story apartments will be built and provided free of cost through the "Amma Illam Scheme." Under this scheme, when Scheduled Caste people living in a joint family, or their sons/daughters get married and establish a separate household, they will be given priority and provided with constructed houses.
points.title: 'Amma Illam Scheme' providing houses for homeless families

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 'Amma Illam Scheme' providing houses for homeless families
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu's Kalaignar Kanavu Illam housing scheme: Everything you need to know
evidence.snippet: The Kalaignar Kanavu Illam Scheme (KKIS) launched in 2024 aims to provide free permanent concrete houses to families in rural areas, with a target of 8 lakh houses by 2030.

plan_existence.notes: The 'Amma Illam Scheme' expands on existing rural housing by promising to purchase land for the landless (current schemes often require the beneficiary to own a patta). It also specifically targets urban multi-story housing and new SC households.

fiscal.comments: The cost of building 1 lakh houses is currently ~Rs 3,500 crore. However, the 'Amma Illam' promise to 'purchase land' for beneficiaries adds a massive, unpredictable cost, especially in urban areas where land prices are prohibitive.

legal.comments: Land acquisition for housing is legally complex and often leads to litigation, potentially delaying the scheme.

administrative.comments: Building multi-story apartments in urban areas and identifying/purchasing land in rural areas requires significant inter-departmental coordination and technical capacity.

timeline.comments: Housing projects, especially those involving land acquisition and multi-story construction, typically take 3-5 years per phase, making a statewide 'homeless-free' goal difficult within one term.

political.comments: Strong appeal, particularly the priority for Scheduled Caste families and the focus on the urban poor.

feasibility.overall_comments: A high-impact social justice initiative that faces severe practical hurdles in land acquisition and urban execution. It is more of a long-term infrastructure goal than a quick-delivery welfare promise.
key_risks: Prohibitive land acquisition costs
Legal delays in land procurement
Construction quality and maintenance of urban apartments

points.text: 100 days employment scheme will be increased to 150 days: The Central Government has announced that the 100 days employment scheme will be increased to 125 days. Steps will be taken to implement this rural development scheme to provide up to 150 days of employment.
points.title: 100 days employment scheme will be increased to 150 days

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 100 days employment scheme will be increased to 150 days
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

existing_scheme_names: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
Viksit Bharat—Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act, 2025

evidence.title: President assents to Viksit Bharat–G RAM G Act, rural employment guarantee raised to 125 Days
evidence.snippet: The legislation replaces MGNREGA and enhances the statutory rural employment guarantee to 125 days per household per financial year.

evidence.title: MNREGA gets new name, 125 days of employment
evidence.snippet: The Union Cabinet approved increasing the guaranteed employment from 100 to 125 days of work per rural household each year.

plan_existence.notes: The Central Government recently (December 2025) increased the statutory limit from 100 to 125 days under the new VB-G RAM G Act. The manifesto promise seeks to expand this further to 150 days, which would require the state to fund the additional 25 days unless the Center grants a specific extension (e.g., for drought).

fiscal.comments: The state would likely have to bear 100% of the wage cost for the additional 25 days (126-150) as the central guarantee ends at 125. With ~91.67 lakh active workers in TN, providing 25 extra days at a wage of ~Rs. 336/day could cost over Rs. 7,000 crore annually if fully utilized.

legal.comments: States are legally permitted to provide employment beyond the central limit using their own funds, as seen in states like Rajasthan and Himachal Pradesh.

administrative.comments: The administrative machinery for MGNREGA is robust and already operational across all village panchayats in Tamil Nadu.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented immediately via a Government Order (GO) and budget allocation.

political.comments: High public appeal in rural areas; aligns with long-standing demands from labor unions and rural activists.

feasibility.overall_comments: Highly feasible administratively but carries a significant fiscal burden that the state must fund independently of the central grant.
key_risks: High fiscal outgo
Potential delays in state-funded wage payments
Verification of work quality for extended periods

points.text: Subsidy scheme for purchasing Amma Two-Wheelers: Amma two-wheelers will be provided to 5 lakh working women with a subsidy of Rs. 25,000/-.
points.title: Subsidy scheme for purchasing Amma Two-Wheelers

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Subsidy scheme for purchasing Amma Two-Wheelers
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Amma Two Wheeler Scheme - Eligibility, Benefits & How to Apply?
evidence.snippet: The scheme provided a 50% subsidy capped at Rs. 25,000 for working women in Tamil Nadu.

evidence.title: How to Apply For Amma Two Wheeler Scheme
evidence.snippet: Recent sources suggest that under the new government (as of 2024), the scheme was scrapped.

plan_existence.notes: The Amma Two Wheeler Scheme was a flagship initiative of the previous AIADMK government but was discontinued/scrapped by the DMK government after 2021. This promise is a reintroduction of the discontinued scheme.

fiscal.comments: The cost for 5 lakh women at Rs. 25,000 each is Rs. 1,250 crore. This is a significant but manageable one-time expenditure for a state budget of TN's size.

legal.comments: No legal or constitutional hurdles; it is a standard welfare subsidy.

administrative.comments: The Tamil Nadu Corporation for Development of Women (TNCDW) has prior experience implementing this exact scheme.

timeline.comments: Can be rolled out in phases over the 5-year term (e.g., 1 lakh women per year).

political.comments: Very high political resonance as it targets a key voting bloc (working women) and revives a popular legacy brand.

feasibility.overall_comments: A well-tested scheme that is highly feasible if the state allocates the necessary ~Rs. 1,250 crore.
key_risks: Budgetary diversion from other schemes
Selection bias in beneficiary identification
Inflation in vehicle prices reducing the impact of the fixed subsidy

points.text: Pensions for the elderly and others increased to Rs. 2,000/-: The monthly social security pension provided to the elderly, widows, destitute unmarried women, deserted women, differently-abled persons, and transgender persons under the social pension scheme will be increased from Rs. 1,200/- to Rs. 2,000/-.
points.title: Pensions for the elderly and others increased to Rs. 2,000/

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Pensions for the elderly and others increased to Rs. 2,000/
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

existing_scheme_names: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu hikes monthly pension to Rs 1,200 from August
evidence.snippet: The state cabinet decided to enhance the monthly pension under various social security schemes to Rs 1,200 from the existing Rs 1,000 in July 2023.

plan_existence.notes: The current pension for most categories (elderly, widows, etc.) is Rs. 1,200, following a hike in 2023. The promise is to increase this to Rs. 2,000.

fiscal.comments: With approximately 30.55 lakh beneficiaries, an increase of Rs. 800 per month (from 1,200 to 2,000) would cost an additional Rs. 2,932 crore annually. This is a recurring and substantial fiscal commitment.

legal.comments: Social security is a state subject; the government has full authority to set pension rates.

administrative.comments: The Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) system for pensions is already well-established in the state.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented immediately upon budget approval.

political.comments: Extremely popular among vulnerable demographics; often a competitive 'bidding' point between parties.

feasibility.overall_comments: Highly feasible administratively but requires significant recurring fiscal space in the state budget.
key_risks: Fiscal sustainability
Inflation eroding the real value of the hike
Exclusion errors in beneficiary lists

points.text: Waiver of education loans obtained from banks: To protect the welfare of students and parents, the government will take over and waive the education loans of those who borrowed from banks but are unable to pay their EMIs due to unemployment.
points.title: Waiver of education loans obtained from banks

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Waiver of education loans obtained from banks
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

existing_scheme_names: Education Loan Waiver for SC/ST/Dalit Christians (2025)

evidence.title: T.N. waives educational loans worth ₹48.95 crore extended to Adi Dravidar, tribal students
evidence.snippet: In February 2025, the TN government waived Rs. 48.95 crore in loans for specific marginalized groups as a special case.

evidence.title: Plea in Madras HC insists on implementing promise to waive education loans
evidence.snippet: A PIL was filed in March 2025 seeking implementation of the 2021 manifesto promise for a general education loan waiver.

plan_existence.notes: While a very limited waiver for SC/ST students was issued in 2025, a general waiver for all unemployed graduates does not exist. Total pending education loans in TN were estimated at over Rs. 16,000 crore in 2025.

fiscal.comments: The total education loan outstanding in TN is approximately Rs. 16,302 crore (as per 2025 reports). Waiving even a significant portion of this would be a massive fiscal shock to the state treasury.

legal.comments: The state cannot 'waive' loans held by Nationalized or Private banks (regulated by RBI). It must 'take over' the debt by paying the banks, which requires complex tripartite agreements.

administrative.comments: Verifying 'inability to pay due to unemployment' is administratively difficult and prone to fraud or disputes.

timeline.comments: Negotiations with banks and verification of lakhs of applicants would take several years.

political.comments: Very high appeal among the youth and middle-class families, but risks backlash from those who already paid their EMIs.

feasibility.overall_comments: Fiscally and legally daunting. While politically attractive, the sheer volume of debt (Rs. 16,000cr+) and the lack of state jurisdiction over commercial banks make this highly improbable to implement fully.
key_risks: Extreme fiscal burden
Legal conflict with RBI/Banking regulations
Moral hazard (encouraging future defaults)
Difficulty in defining and verifying 'unemployment'

points.text: 3 free gas cylinders per year as livelihood assistance: As livelihood assistance, 3 free gas cylinders will be provided annually to the family of Rice Cardholders.
points.title: 3 free gas cylinders per year as livelihood assistance

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 free gas cylinders per year as livelihood assistance
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Andhra cabinet approves 3 free LPG cylinders from Diwali
evidence.snippet: The Andhra Pradesh state cabinet decided to roll out the 'Deepam' scheme, through which three LPG cylinders will be supplied to poor households per year free of cost.

evidence.title: AIADMK Promises Free Refrigerators, LPG Cylinders In Tamil Nadu Manifesto
evidence.snippet: The party announced three free LPG cylinders yearly and cash transfer schemes for households, according to the 2026 manifesto.

plan_existence.notes: While the Central Government's PMUY provides subsidies, no current Tamil Nadu state scheme provides three entirely free cylinders annually. Andhra Pradesh recently implemented a near-identical scheme (Deepam 2.0) costing approximately Rs. 2,684 crore per year.

fiscal.comments: With approximately 2.22 crore rice cardholders in Tamil Nadu, providing 3 cylinders at ~Rs. 850 each would cost roughly Rs. 5,660 crore annually. This is a massive recurring expenditure for a state already facing high debt.

legal.comments: No significant legal hurdles as it is a welfare subsidy delivered via DBT or PDS.

administrative.comments: Highly feasible using the existing PDS (Smart Ration Card) database and Oil Marketing Company (OMC) networks.

timeline.comments: Can be rolled out within the first year of governance, as seen in Andhra Pradesh.

political.comments: Extremely high public appeal; aligns with competitive populism seen in neighboring states.

feasibility.overall_comments: Technically and administratively simple but fiscally draining. It is a direct response to similar successful promises in other South Indian states.
key_risks: High fiscal burden
Potential for leakage in the PDS-LPG linkage
Inflation in gas prices increasing state liability

points.text: • Rs. 10 lakh compensation for families of Jallikattu tamers: If Jallikattu tamers unfortunately lose their lives while taming bulls during the traditional Tamil martial sport of Jallikattu, their families will be provided with Rs. 10 lakh. Similarly, Rs. 2 lakh will be provided for medical treatment to those who sustain severe injuries. • A Government subsidy of Rs. 5 lakh will be provided to enthusiastically organize Jallikattu events in places where government orders have been issued. • The online registration system currently followed for organizing Jallikattu events will be cancelled. The previously existing manual system will be reinstated.
points.title: Rs. 10 lakh compensation for families of Jallikattu tamers
bullets: Rs. 10 lakh compensation for families of Jallikattu tamers: If Jallikattu tamers unfortunately lose their lives while taming bulls during the traditional Tamil martial sport of Jallikattu, their families will be provided with Rs. 10 lakh. Similarly, Rs. 2 lakh will be provided for medical treatment to those who sustain severe injuries.
A Government subsidy of Rs. 5 lakh will be provided to enthusiastically organize Jallikattu events in places where government orders have been issued.
The online registration system currently followed for organizing Jallikattu events will be cancelled. The previously existing manual system will be reinstated.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Rs. 10 lakh compensation for families of Jallikattu tamers
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

existing_scheme_names: Chief Minister's Public Relief Fund (CMPRF) solatium

evidence.title: CM Stalin announces Rs 3 lakh to families of 2 men killed in Jallikattu events
evidence.snippet: Chief Minister MK Stalin has announced a solatium of Rs 3 lakh to the families of the deceased bull tamers.

evidence.title: EPS promises Rs 10 lakh ex gratia in case of Jallikattu fatalities
evidence.snippet: AIADMK General Secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami announced an ex gratia compensation of Rs 10 lakh for registered Jallikattu players who lose their lives.

plan_existence.notes: Compensation currently exists but at a lower rate (typically Rs. 3 lakh). The promise to provide a Rs. 5 lakh subsidy for event organization and reverting to a manual registration system are new/reversal components.

fiscal.comments: The number of fatalities in Jallikattu is relatively low (typically under 20 per year), making the Rs. 10 lakh compensation and event subsidies fiscally negligible for the state budget.

legal.comments: Reverting to a manual registration system may violate safety and transparency guidelines mandated by the Supreme Court and the Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) which favor digital tracking for fitness and safety.

administrative.comments: Reverting to a manual system is a step backward in administrative efficiency and could lead to corruption or lack of accountability in participant safety checks.

timeline.comments: Compensation and subsidies can be implemented immediately via executive order.

political.comments: Strongly appeals to the rural heartland and cultural pride, particularly in the 'Jallikattu belt' (Madurai, Sivaganga, Pudukkottai).

feasibility.overall_comments: The welfare components are highly feasible and popular, but the administrative reversal of the registration system is regressive and legally risky.
key_risks: Legal challenges from animal rights groups
Safety risks due to manual registration
Potential for fraudulent injury claims

evidence.title: A BRIEF NOTE ON TAMCO - Government of Tamil Nadu
evidence.snippet: The Objective of TAMCO is to provide loans at low rate of interest (6% to 8%) to Minorities for Self-Employment and income generation activities.

plan_existence.notes: The Tamil Nadu Minorities Economic Development Corporation (TAMCO) already provides loans, but they currently carry an interest rate of 6% to 8%. Making them 'interest-free' would require the state to subsidize the entire interest component.

fiscal.comments: The state would need to bear the interest burden (6-8%) on loans. Given TAMCO's typical annual disbursement (e.g., ~Rs. 6.72 crore in Coimbatore over 3 years), the statewide interest subsidy would be manageable.

legal.comments: Targeting religious minorities for economic development through state corporations is legally established and constitutional under Article 15(4).

administrative.comments: Can be easily implemented by modifying the existing TAMCO and District Central Cooperative Bank (DCCB) lending protocols.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented within 6-12 months by issuing a Government Order (GO) for interest subvention.

political.comments: Strongly appeals to minority vote banks; however, may face criticism regarding 'appeasement' from opposition parties.

feasibility.overall_comments: A highly feasible expansion of an existing mechanism that significantly lowers the barrier for minority women entrepreneurs.
key_risks: Potential for high NPA (Non-Performing Assets) if repayment discipline is not maintained
Limited reach if TAMCO's corpus is not significantly increased

points.text: Loans obtained by differently-abled persons from cooperative banks will be waived.
points.title: Loans obtained by differently-abled persons from cooperative banks will be waived.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Loans obtained by differently-abled persons from cooperative banks will be waived.
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

existing_scheme_names: Loans to Physically Challenged Persons (TNSC Bank)

evidence.title: Will waive cooperative society loans for persons with disabilities, promises Palaniswami
evidence.snippet: AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami announced that loans taken by persons with disabilities from cooperative societies would be waived.

evidence.title: TN govt waives Rs 6,000 cr cooperative bank gold loans
evidence.snippet: A total of 13.12 lakh people benefitted through Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin's gesture of waiving Rs 6,000 crore cooperative bank loans.

plan_existence.notes: While cooperative banks currently offer specialized loans for the differently-abled at 4-6% interest, a blanket waiver of the principal and interest is a new proposal. There is strong precedent for large-scale cooperative loan waivers in Tamil Nadu.

legal.comments: The state government has full authority over cooperative banks and can authorize waivers through budgetary allocations.

administrative.comments: Cooperative banks have robust records of their borrowers; identifying and waiving loans for those with valid disability certificates is administratively straightforward.

timeline.comments: Can be executed within the first 100 days of a new government, as is traditional for loan waivers in TN.

political.comments: High moral and political ground; difficult for any opposition to criticize a welfare measure for the differently-abled.

feasibility.overall_comments: A highly popular and administratively feasible social justice measure with strong local precedent, though the total fiscal impact remains unquantified without current outstanding loan data.
key_risks: Moral hazard (encouraging future defaults)
Impact on the liquidity of cooperative banks if state reimbursement is delayed

points.text: A monthly assistance of Rs. 2,000 will be provided to unemployed graduate youth and young women registered in employment exchanges; Rs. 1,000 per month will be provided to those who have studied up to +2 and registered in employment exchanges.
points.title: A monthly assistance of Rs. 2,000 will be provided to unemployed graduate youth and young women registered in employment

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A monthly assistance of Rs. 2,000 will be provided to unemployed graduate youth and young women registered in employment
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

existing_scheme_names: Unemployment Assistance Scheme for Educated Youth (UAEY)

evidence.title: Unemployment Scholarship in Tamil Nadu – Complete Guide (2026)
evidence.snippet: The Tamil Nadu government offers a monthly financial allowance: Graduate (Degree) - ₹600, 12th Pass - ₹400, 10th Pass - ₹300.

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu Unemployment Assistance Scheme
evidence.snippet: The scheme provides monthly assistance to unemployed youth registered in employment exchanges for at least 5 years.

plan_existence.notes: The existing scheme provides significantly lower amounts (Rs 600 for graduates vs the promised Rs 2,000). The promise represents a 233% increase for graduates and a 150% increase for HSC holders.

fiscal.comments: With an unemployment rate for female graduates at 24.3% in TN (Economic Survey 2024-25), the fiscal burden of tripling the allowance for millions of registered youth is substantial. No specific outlay is provided in the promise.

legal.comments: Purely a state welfare policy; no legal or constitutional hurdles.

administrative.comments: The infrastructure (Employment Exchanges and DBT) already exists, making the delivery mechanism highly feasible.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented immediately via a Government Order (GO) as it builds on an existing scheme.

political.comments: Highly popular among the youth and first-time voters; low risk of opposition.

feasibility.overall_comments: The promise is a significant expansion of an existing, well-oiled administrative mechanism. While the fiscal cost is high due to the volume of unemployed graduates, it is a standard welfare tool used by state governments.
key_risks: High fiscal burden on state exchequer
Potential for fraudulent registrations in employment exchanges
Inflationary pressure from large-scale cash transfers

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu government announces ₹3000 Pongal gift for all rice ration card-holders

plan_existence.notes: The cash component varies annually. In 2026, it was Rs 3,000; in 2024 and 2023, it was Rs 1,000. The promise formalizes the Rs 1,000 amount.

administrative.comments: The PDS network is highly efficient in Tamil Nadu for distributing both hampers and cash.

political.comments: While popular, the promise of Rs 1,000 might be perceived as a reduction if compared to the 2026 pre-election payout of Rs 3,000.

Political backlash if the amount is lower than previous years

points.text: Loans obtained from cooperative banks by small street vendors operating on pavements in urban areas will be waived.
points.title: Loans obtained from cooperative banks by small street vendors operating on pavements in urban areas will be waived.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Loans obtained from cooperative banks by small street vendors operating on pavements in urban areas will be waived.
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: PM SVANidhi Loan Guide for Street Vendors
evidence.snippet: PM SVANidhi provides collateral-free working capital loans of up to ₹50,000 to street vendors with interest subsidies, but does not include a loan waiver component.

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu Cooperative Bank Schemes
evidence.snippet: Tamil Nadu cooperative banks offer various micro-credit schemes for small traders and street vendors, but current policies focus on disbursement and interest subvention rather than total waiver.

plan_existence.notes: While credit facilities like PM SVANidhi (Central) and state-level cooperative loans exist to provide liquidity to street vendors, there is currently no active scheme that waives the principal or interest for these specific urban informal workers. Previous waivers in Tamil Nadu have primarily targeted farmers and Women Self-Help Groups (SHGs).

legal.comments: State governments have the authority to waive loans held by state-controlled cooperative banks, though it requires legislative approval and budgetary allocation.

administrative.comments: Identifying 'small street vendors' specifically in urban areas is complex; while many are registered under the Street Vendors Act, a large portion remains in the informal, unregistered sector.

timeline.comments: A loan waiver can be executed via a single government order and bank reconciliation within the first year of a term.

political.comments: Highly popular among the urban poor and informal labor unions; carries low political risk but may face criticism regarding credit discipline.

feasibility.overall_comments: The promise is legally feasible but lacks fiscal clarity due to the absence of public data on the total outstanding debt of urban street vendors in the cooperative sector. It addresses a significant debt trap in the informal economy.
key_risks: Erosion of credit culture in cooperative banking
Difficulty in verifying 'small' vendor status for unregistered individuals
Potential for fraudulent claims by non-vendors

evidence.title: 644 women auto drivers benefited with ₹1 lakh each as subsidy
evidence.snippet: The Tamil Nadu government currently provides a ₹1 lakh subsidy specifically for women auto drivers to purchase new vehicles.

evidence.title: AIADMK promises Rs 75,000 subsidy for auto drivers
evidence.snippet: The AIADMK has promised a Rs 75,000 subsidy for auto drivers to purchase new vehicles in its 2026 election outreach.

plan_existence.notes: Current subsidies are highly targeted (e.g., women drivers or electric vehicle conversions). A universal ₹75,000 subsidy for all non-owner registered association members would be a significant expansion of the existing welfare board's scope.

fiscal.comments: With an estimated 2.5 to 3 lakh auto rickshaws in Tamil Nadu, providing ₹75,000 to even half that number (non-owners) would cost approximately ₹1,125 crore, a substantial one-time outlay.

legal.comments: No legal barriers; the state can provide subsidies through the Tamil Nadu Auto Rickshaw Drivers Welfare Board.

administrative.comments: The existing Welfare Board and the Auto Drivers Association provide a ready database for identification, though verifying 'non-ownership' requires cross-referencing RTO data.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented in phases over a 5-year term, starting with the oldest registered members.

political.comments: Auto drivers are a highly organized and politically influential voting bloc in Tamil Nadu; this promise has high electoral resonance.

feasibility.overall_comments: A high-impact, politically astute promise that is administratively feasible but requires careful fiscal management to avoid straining the transport department's budget.
key_risks: Fiscal burden of over ₹1,000 crore
Potential for corruption in the 'non-owner' verification process
Market saturation of autos leading to reduced earnings for existing drivers

existing_scheme_names: Free Saree and Dhoti Distribution Scheme

evidence.title: TN govt releases Rs 100 cr installment for free veshti, sarees during Pongal 2025
evidence.snippet: The Tamil Nadu government distributes free dhotis and sarees to 1.77 crore beneficiaries annually for the Pongal festival.

evidence.title: For Pongal 2024, 1.77 crore sarees and dhoties were distributed at a cost of Rs.449.47 crore
evidence.snippet: The scheme provides continuous employment to weavers and fulfills clothing needs for 3.50 crore people.

plan_existence.notes: The existing scheme is tied to the Pongal festival. Expanding this to Deepavali effectively doubles the annual requirement and cost. The promise also emphasizes 'high-quality,' which implies a potential increase in the per-unit procurement cost.

fiscal.comments: The current Pongal scheme costs approximately ₹450-500 crore annually. Doubling this for Deepavali would require an additional ₹500 crore per year, totaling ~₹1,000 crore annually for both festivals.

legal.comments: No legal hurdles; it is a standard welfare procurement program.

administrative.comments: The state has a robust mechanism through Co-optex and the PDS network to manufacture and distribute these items, as demonstrated for decades during Pongal.

timeline.comments: Can be implemented immediately in the first festive cycle after taking office.

political.comments: Popular welfare measure that also supports the local powerloom and handloom weaver vote bank.

feasibility.overall_comments: Highly feasible as it leverages existing infrastructure. The primary challenge is the recurring fiscal cost and ensuring the 'high-quality' claim is met to avoid public dissatisfaction.
key_risks: Procurement delays leading to late distribution
Quality control issues in mass production
Increased fiscal deficit due to recurring expenditure

points.text: To reduce the household workload of homemakers, a free refrigerator (Fridge) will be provided to rice family cardholders.
points.title: To reduce the household workload of homemakers, a free refrigerator (Fridge) will be provided to rice family cardholders

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: To reduce the household workload of homemakers, a free refrigerator (Fridge) will be provided to rice family cardholders
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu has 2.22 crore active family ration cards
evidence.snippet: Official records show Tamil Nadu has 2.22 crore active family ration cards, with the majority being rice cards.

plan_existence.notes: While Tamil Nadu has a history of distributing free consumer durables (TVs, mixers, grinders, fans), a free refrigerator scheme has never been implemented. This would be a first-of-its-kind large-scale durable distribution.

fiscal.comments: With 2.22 crore rice cardholders, even a basic refrigerator costing ₹10,000 would require a staggering ₹22,200 crore. This is nearly 10% of the state's total annual revenue receipts, making it fiscally extreme.

legal.comments: No legal barriers to state-funded welfare distribution.

administrative.comments: Logistically massive; transporting and delivering 2.2 crore refrigerators to every household, including remote rural areas, would take years and face significant storage and breakage risks.

timeline.comments: Unlikely to be completed for all 2.2 crore beneficiaries within a single 5-year term given the manufacturing and supply chain constraints.

political.comments: Extremely high populist appeal, but risks backlash if distribution is slow or if electricity bills for poor households spike due to fridge usage.

feasibility.overall_comments: While politically attractive, the scheme is fiscally reckless and administratively nightmarish. The cost exceeds almost all other individual welfare schemes in the state's history.
key_risks: Severe fiscal strain on the state exchequer
Massive logistical and corruption risks in procurement/delivery
Increased electricity demand and costs for BPL families
Environmental impact of disposing of 22 million units eventually

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu Govt decides to keep special PDS intact
evidence.snippet: The state government provides 1kg of tur dal at Rs 30 and 1 litre of palmolein oil at Rs 25 to ration cardholders. Rice is already provided free of cost.

evidence.title: PDS subsidy for four-member family in TN at a high of Rs 300
evidence.snippet: The state government has earmarked Rs 4,800 crore annually for supplying tur dal and palmolein oil alone, out of the total budget of Rs 13,000 crore for the Food department for 2025-26.

plan_existence.notes: Currently, Tamil Nadu provides free rice, but dal and oil are sold at highly subsidized rates (Rs 30/kg and Rs 25/litre). The promise seeks to eliminate the remaining nominal cost, making them entirely free.

fiscal.comments: The state currently collects approximately Rs 1,200 to Rs 1,300 crore annually from the sale of subsidized dal and oil. Making them free would increase the annual food subsidy bill (currently ~Rs 13,000 crore) by this amount. While significant, it is manageable within the state's welfare budget.

legal.comments: The state has full jurisdiction over its Special PDS and can modify pricing or provide items for free under its executive powers.

administrative.comments: The infrastructure for distribution (35,000+ fair price shops) and procurement (TNCSC) is already robust and fully operational.

timeline.comments: This can be implemented immediately upon the first cabinet meeting of a new government through a Government Order (GO).

political.comments: Highly popular welfare measure with no significant political opposition; aligns with the state's long-standing 'Universal PDS' policy.

feasibility.overall_comments: The plan is highly feasible as it utilizes existing infrastructure. The primary challenge is the recurring fiscal burden of roughly Rs 1,300 crore annually in lost revenue, but this is a standard feature of Tamil Nadu's welfare-centric fiscal policy.
key_risks: Fluctuations in global commodity prices for palm oil and pulses increasing procurement costs.
Potential for leakage or black marketing if monitoring is not tightened.
Increased fiscal deficit if not balanced by other revenue streams.

points.text: According to Perarignar Anna's principle, "We will extend our hand for friendship, and raise our voice for rights" is the guiding mantra of the All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam. There is no denying that for India to shine as a strong superpower, the Central Government must be strong. At the same time, State Governments must also function independently with appropriate financial and administrative rights. Accordingly, it is our policy that the Central Government must provide proper financial and administrative devolution to the State Governments and this will pressed always.
points.title: According to Perarignar Anna's principle, "We will extend our hand for friendship, and raise our voice for rights" is th

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: According to Perarignar Anna's principle, "We will extend our hand for friendship, and raise our voice for rights" is th
Central, State Federal System
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: AIADMK 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly Election Manifesto
evidence.snippet: The manifesto emphasizes federal rights and administrative reforms, continuing the party's long-standing stance on state autonomy.

plan_existence.notes: This is a foundational political philosophy of the AIADMK (and other Dravidian parties) rather than a specific scheme. It represents an ongoing policy stance regarding India's federal structure.

legal.comments: Changes to financial and administrative devolution often require constitutional amendments or shifts in the Finance Commission's terms of reference, which are outside the state's unilateral control.

administrative.comments: The state has the administrative capacity to manage more powers if devolved, but the actual transfer depends on Central Government cooperation.

timeline.comments: Achieving significant shifts in federal devolution is a long-term political process that rarely concludes within a single 5-year term.

political.comments: Strong public support in Tamil Nadu for state rights; however, it requires building a coalition with other states to pressure the Central Government.

feasibility.overall_comments: This is a statement of political intent and policy stance rather than a time-bound project. Its success depends entirely on the political climate at the Center and the state's bargaining power in the federal setup.
key_risks: Resistance from the Central Government to cede administrative or financial control.
Constitutional limitations on state powers.
Lack of consensus among other Indian states on specific devolution formulas.

points.text: To protect the rights of the states, within the framework of the Indian Constitution, our stance is that along with the authoritative responsibilities granted to the states, the necessary financial allocations and responsibilities must be properly provided for the State Governments to function independently. Therefore, we will insist that additional financial devolution be provided to the states in accordance with changing circumstances.
points.title: To protect the rights of the states, within the framework of the Indian Constitution, our stance is that along with the

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: To protect the rights of the states, within the framework of the Indian Constitution, our stance is that along with the
Central, State Federal System
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Stalin demands 50% share of central taxes for Tamil Nadu
evidence.snippet: Tamil Nadu has consistently urged the Finance Commission to raise the state's share of central taxes to 50%.

plan_existence.notes: The demand for additional financial devolution is a persistent stance of the Tamil Nadu government across different administrations (both DMK and AIADMK).

fiscal.comments: The fiscal impact would be a massive increase in state revenue if successful, but the state cannot unilaterally implement this.

legal.comments: Financial devolution is governed by Article 280 of the Constitution; states can only 'insist' or 'demand' through the Finance Commission process.

administrative.comments: The state finance department is well-equipped to manage increased allocations and has a clear roadmap for utilizing additional funds.

timeline.comments: Depends on the cycle of the Finance Commission (currently the 16th FC for the 2026-2031 period).

political.comments: High domestic political resonance as it frames the state as a victim of central neglect.

feasibility.overall_comments: This is a standard advocacy point in Tamil Nadu politics. While administratively feasible to manage more funds, the actual realization of 'additional devolution' is a matter of central-state negotiation and constitutional commission recommendations.
key_risks: Finance Commission formula favoring 'equity' (poorer states) over 'efficiency' (developed states like TN).
Central government's increasing use of cesses and surcharges which are not part of the divisible pool.

points.text: Pointing out that the Central Finance Commissions formed after the 12th Finance Commission have gradually reduced the financial allocation for Tamil Nadu, we continuously insist that financial devolution should be structured in a way that does not adversely affect developed states like Tamil Nadu. Standing firm on this stance, we will demand that Tamil Nadu receives its financial share without any reduction.
points.title: Pointing out that the Central Finance Commissions formed after the 12th Finance Commission have gradually reduced the fi

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Pointing out that the Central Finance Commissions formed after the 12th Finance Commission have gradually reduced the fi
Central, State Federal System

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu saw only a minimal rise in share from 4.079% to 4.097%
evidence.snippet: The 16th Finance Commission retained the states' share at 41%. Tamil Nadu's share remains low due to criteria like demographic performance and income distance.

plan_existence.notes: Tamil Nadu's share has historically declined from 7.931% (9th FC) to around 4.08% (15th/16th FC). The promise is to fight this trend.

fiscal.comments: The state claims a cumulative loss of Rs 3.17 lakh crore due to reduced shares since the 9th Finance Commission.

legal.comments: The Finance Commission is an independent constitutional body; its recommendations are generally accepted by the Center, and states have limited legal recourse to change the formula once set.

administrative.comments: The state government consistently prepares high-quality memoranda for the Finance Commission to argue its case.

timeline.comments: The 16th Finance Commission's recommendations cover the period up to 2031, coinciding with the 2026-2031 state government term.

political.comments: This is a core grievance used to mobilize voters against central overreach and to explain state fiscal constraints.

feasibility.overall_comments: This is a reactive policy stance. While the state will certainly 'demand' a higher share, the outcome is determined by a federal body (Finance Commission) using a formula that currently penalizes states with successful population control and higher GSDP.
key_risks: Continued use of 2011 census data which disadvantages southern states.
Weightage for 'income distance' continuing to favor less developed northern states.
Political friction with the Center if the demand is framed as 'North vs South'.

existing_scheme_names: Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly Resolution against Delimitation (2024)

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly passes resolutions against delimitation and 'one nation, one election'
evidence.snippet: The Tamil Nadu Assembly on February 14, 2024, passed a resolution against the proposed delimitation process after 2026, describing it as a 'sword hanging over the head of Tamil Nadu'.

plan_existence.notes: The Tamil Nadu government has already formally adopted this stance through a unanimous assembly resolution in February 2024, urging the Union government to maintain the 1971 census ratio for seat allocation.

legal.comments: Delimitation is a Union subject under Article 82 of the Constitution; a state can only advocate or pass non-binding resolutions.

administrative.comments: The state has already demonstrated the capacity to pass resolutions and engage in political advocacy on this issue.

timeline.comments: Advocacy is ongoing and can be sustained throughout the 5-year term.

political.comments: High consensus across all major regional parties in Tamil Nadu as it protects the state's political weight.

feasibility.overall_comments: This is a political advocacy promise rather than a deliverable scheme. While the state can 'insist', the final authority rests with the Union Parliament and the Delimitation Commission.
key_risks: Constitutional mandate for population-based delimitation
Lack of consensus among other Indian states
Limited legal recourse for the state government

points.text: Our stance remains that crucial departments aiding human resource development, such as Education and Medicine, must be transferred from the Concurrent List to the State List of the Indian Constitution. We will continuously urge the Central Government to transfer these to the State List.

existing_scheme_names: State Policy Stance on Seventh Schedule

evidence.title: In NEET row, TN harks back to an old debate: should education be in Concurrent List?
evidence.snippet: Chief Minister M K Stalin has repeatedly called for education to be moved from the Concurrent list back to the State list to allow states to abolish exams like NEET.

plan_existence.notes: The demand to move Education and Medicine (specifically medical education) back to the State List has been a core policy stance of the DMK-led government, especially in the context of the NEET controversy.

legal.comments: Requires a Constitutional Amendment under Article 368, needing a two-thirds majority in both Houses of Parliament and ratification by at least half of the State Legislatures.

administrative.comments: If transferred, the state has the existing department infrastructure to manage these sectors independently.

timeline.comments: Extremely unlikely to be achieved within 5 years given the current national political landscape and the need for central cooperation.

political.comments: Faces significant opposition from the Union Government which favors national standards (like NEP and NEET).

feasibility.overall_comments: This is a long-standing ideological demand with very low legal feasibility as it requires the Union Government to voluntarily cede power through a difficult constitutional process.
key_risks: Constitutional amendment hurdles
Conflict with National Education Policy (NEP)
Judicial challenges regarding national standards

points.text: In Central tax collection, surcharges and cesses are not included in the divisible pool shared with State Governments; the Central Government collects and utilizes them entirely. existing_scheme_names: State Representation to the 16th Finance Commission

evidence.title: Tamil Nadu demands merger of cesses and surcharges
evidence.snippet: Tamil Nadu has consistently demanded the inclusion of cesses and surcharges in the divisible pool, noting they have grown to over 20% of the Centre's gross tax revenue.

plan_existence.notes: The 16th Finance Commission (2026-2031) is currently the primary forum where this demand is being raised by Tamil Nadu and other states.

fiscal.comments: If successful, this would significantly increase state revenue, but the cost to the Union is the primary barrier.

legal.comments: Requires an amendment to Article 270/271 of the Constitution to redefine the divisible pool.

administrative.comments: Implementation would be handled through the existing tax devolution mechanism of the Finance Commission.

timeline.comments: Depends on the recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission and subsequent Union action during the 2026-2031 period.

political.comments: Strong support from most states (including non-opposition ones), but strong resistance from the Union Finance Ministry.

feasibility.overall_comments: A critical fiscal federalism issue. While the state can 'press' the issue, the outcome depends entirely on a Constitutional change or a shift in the Finance Commission's mandate.
key_risks: Union government's reliance on cess for specific schemes
Constitutional barriers
Resistance from the Union to share non-divisible revenue

points.text: To ensure that the public quickly receives essential documents from the government—such as community certificates, birth and death certificates, income certificates, nativity certificates, patta transfers, pension benefits, and public distribution system benefits—within specified days after applying, infrastructure like public service centers (E-Seva) will be strengthened.

Public Administration
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: e-Sevai - Tamil Nadu Information Technology Department
evidence.snippet: The Government of Tamil Nadu has proposed to roll out 5440 CSCs throughout the State to provide G2C and B2C services.

evidence.title: TNeGA asks Chennai Corporation to restore e-seva centres in ward offices
evidence.snippet: TNeGA is working to restore and expand e-seva centres in ward offices to ensure public access to essential documents.

plan_existence.notes: The e-Sevai system is well-established in Tamil Nadu. The promise focuses on 'strengthening' and ensuring 'specified days' for delivery, which aligns with existing Right to Service goals.

fiscal.comments: Strengthening existing infrastructure is fiscally manageable within the state's IT and e-governance budget.

legal.comments: No legal hurdles; falls entirely within the state's administrative powers.

administrative.comments: TNeGA has a strong track record, though improving speed and reducing corruption at the local level remains a challenge.

timeline.comments: Infrastructure upgrades and process improvements can be completed within 1-3 years.

political.comments: Highly popular as it reduces bureaucratic friction for common citizens.

feasibility.overall_comments: A highly feasible and practical promise that builds on an existing, successful digital infrastructure. Success depends on strict enforcement of service timelines.
key_risks: Local level corruption/bribery
Technical downtime in rural areas
Staffing and training gaps at new centers

points.text: Petitions received from the public through various channels like Manu Neethi Naal, grievance redressal meetings, and the Chief Minister's special cell will be properly reviewed. The website will be upgraded so that the public can directly know all the decisions taken. Through this, the public can track the actions taken on the petitions they submitted.

Public Administration
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: Mudhalvarin Mugavari Portal
evidence.snippet: An integrated public grievance redressal platform where citizens can file and track grievances via multiple channels including web, app, and helpline.

evidence.title: New grievance redressal system out - The Hindu
evidence.snippet: The Integrated and Inclusive Public Grievance CM Helpline Management System was launched in 2021 to streamline petitions from various sources.

plan_existence.notes: Tamil Nadu already has a robust integrated grievance system called 'Mudhalvarin Mugavari' that allows tracking. The promise to upgrade the website for direct visibility of decisions is an expansion of the current portal's transparency features.

fiscal.comments: Upgrading an existing web portal and enhancing data visualization is a low-cost IT project that fits within the existing administrative budget.

legal.comments: The state government has full authority to modify its internal grievance redressal mechanisms and public-facing portals.

administrative.comments: The Mudhalvarin Mugavari Department and TNeGA already manage the infrastructure; adding more granular tracking is a routine technical update.

timeline.comments: Portal upgrades and backend integration can be fully implemented within the first 6-12 months of the term.

political.comments: Improving transparency in grievance redressal is a popular, non-partisan governance improvement with no significant opposition.

feasibility.overall_comments: Highly feasible as it builds on existing digital infrastructure. It requires minimal fiscal outlay and leverages established administrative departments.
key_risks: Ensuring all departments consistently update the 'action taken' status in real-time
Maintaining data privacy while increasing transparency of decisions

points.text: Severe action will be taken against officials and ministers who have engaged in deep-rooted corruption across departments over the past 5 years, looting the public's tax money. Regarding this, complaints already submitted to the Governor will be reviewed, and appropriate legal action will be initiated against the concerned individuals.

Public Administration
AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026

evidence.title: AIADMK Alleges Rs 4 Lakh Crore Corruption by DMK
evidence.snippet: In January 2026, the AIADMK submitted a memorandum to the Governor alleging massive corruption in over 20 government departments over the previous five years.

evidence.title: HC Orders DVAC To File FIR Against TN Minister
evidence.snippet: The Madras High Court directed the DVAC to register an FIR against a sitting minister in February 2026 following corruption allegations.

plan_existence.notes: Anti-corruption mechanisms like the DVAC and Lokayukta are already active. The promise specifically targets the 2021-2026 period and refers to specific complaints already lodged with the Governor by the opposition party.

legal.comments: Prosecuting ministers and high-level officials requires statutory sanctions (e.g., Section 19 of the PC Act) and is often delayed by judicial interventions and stay orders.

administrative.comments: The DVAC is a capable investigative body, but its effectiveness in high-profile cases depends on its operational independence from the executive.

timeline.comments: Corruption cases in India typically take a decade or more to reach conviction; completing 'severe action' within a 5-year term is highly unlikely.

political.comments: Such targeted investigations are frequently challenged as political vendettas, which can lead to significant political friction and public polarization.

feasibility.overall_comments: While the administrative machinery exists, the legal hurdles and the slow pace of the Indian judicial system make it difficult to deliver 'severe action' within a single term. The promise is largely a continuation of existing legal processes with a specific political focus.
key_risks: Legal challenges and stay orders from the High Court/Supreme Court
Difficulty in proving 'deep-rooted' corruption with admissible evidence
Accusations of political vendetta undermining the credibility of the investigations
